title: Krunker.io: Complete Guide to Mastering the Blocky FPS
excerpt: Learn everything about Krunker.io - from basic controls to advanced combat strategies. Start dominating the arena today.
content:
Krunker.io is one of the most popular browser FPS games, known for its blocky pixelated graphics and fast-paced gameplay. With a huge active community and multiple game modes, it's the go-to shooter for browser gamers.

## Getting Started

When you first join a match, you'll spawn with a default weapon class. Your goal is to eliminate other players, earn points, and climb the leaderboard. The game features multiple maps inspired by popular FPS games.

## Controls

- WASD: Move
- Mouse: Aim
- Left Click: Shoot
- R: Reload
- Shift: Crouch
- Space: Jump
- Tab: View scoreboard

## Weapon Classes

Krunker.io offers several weapon classes, each with unique characteristics:

- **Assault Rifle**: Balanced damage and fire rate, great for medium range
- **Sniper**: One-shot potential but requires precise aim
- **SMG**: High fire rate, ideal for close-quarters combat
- **Shotgun**: Devastating up close, useless at range
- **Revolver**: High damage per shot, rewards accuracy

## Advanced Strategies

1. **Movement is Key**: Never stand still. Use bunny hopping to move quickly while being harder to hit
2. **Map Knowledge**: Learn spawn points, cover locations, and sightlines
3. **Crosshair Placement**: Keep your crosshair at head height for faster kills
4. **Slide Hopping**: Advanced technique to maintain momentum while moving

## Game Modes

Krunker.io features several game modes including Free For All, Team Deathmatch, Hardpoint, and Capture the Flag. Each mode requires different strategies.

## Conclusion

Krunker.io rewards aggressive but smart play. Master the movement mechanics, learn the maps, and you'll be topping the leaderboard in no time. Play Krunker.io free on PlayOnHub!

title: Vex 7: 10 Tips to Beat the Hardest Levels
excerpt: Stuck on a difficult Vex 7 stage? These tips and tricks will help you overcome the most challenging obstacle courses in this stickman platformer.
content:
Vex 7 is the latest installment in the legendary stickman platformer series, known for its brutal difficulty and deadly obstacle courses. Each level is designed to test your reflexes, timing, and patience. But with the right approach, you can conquer them all. Here are 10 essential tips.

## 1. Patience is Key

Don't rush into obstacles. Each trap has a pattern, and rushing leads to unnecessary deaths. Take a moment to observe saw blades, spikes, and moving platforms before making your move.

## 2. Master Wall Jumping

Wall jumping is essential in Vex 7. Press jump while sliding against a wall to rebound to higher platforms. Practice this in early levels - you'll need it constantly in harder stages.

## 3. Learn Trap Patterns

Most traps repeat their patterns. Observe the timing of saw blades and moving spikes before attempting a section. Once you know the rhythm, execution becomes much easier.

## 4. Use Checkpoints

Vex 7 auto-saves at checkpoints marked by flags. Don't worry about dying - each attempt teaches you something new about the level layout.

## 5. Watch for Hidden Spikes

Small visual cues often hint at upcoming traps. Pay attention to subtle cracks in the floor or slightly different colored tiles - they often hide deadly spikes.

## 6. Swim Efficiently

In water sections, tap the up key instead of holding it for more controlled swimming. Look for air bubbles to plan your underwater route.

## 7. Slide Under Barriers

Use the slide move (down arrow or S) to pass under low barriers quickly. Many players forget this move and try to jump over everything.

## 8. Watch Speedruns

YouTube speedruns show optimal routes and strategies for Vex 7. Even if you can't replicate their speed, the route knowledge is invaluable for finding hidden paths.

## 9. Take Breaks

Frustration leads to mistakes. If you're stuck on a level for too long, take a 5-minute break and come back fresh. Your muscle memory will improve even during breaks.

## 10. Celebrate Small Wins

Each level you beat in Vex 7 is an achievement. Don't get discouraged by the next challenge - you're making progress with every attempt.

## Conclusion

Vex 7 is designed to be challenging but fair. Every death teaches you something about timing, positioning, or level layout. Keep trying, practice your wall jumps, and you'll eventually beat every obstacle course. Play Vex 7 free on PlayOnHub!

title: Slope: How to Get Insanely High Scores
excerpt: Master Slope with these pro strategies. Learn how to survive longer, react faster, and achieve record-breaking scores.
content:
Slope is deceptively simple: steer a ball down a neon slope and avoid obstacles. But as the speed increases, only the most skilled players survive. Here's how to get high scores.

## Understanding the Speed Curve

Slope starts slow but accelerates continuously. The first 30 seconds are easy, but after a minute, the ball moves incredibly fast. Your reaction time needs to scale with the speed.

## Core Strategies

- **Stay Centered**: The center of the slope gives you the most options for dodging. Only move to the sides when necessary.
- **Small Movements**: Sharp turns can send you off the edge. Make small, deliberate adjustments.
- **Look Ahead**: Don't focus on the ball - look at what's coming next. Anticipation beats reaction.
- **Stay Calm**: As speed increases, panic is your biggest enemy. Keep your movements smooth and controlled.

## Advanced Techniques

### The Drift Technique

When approaching a wall obstacle at high speed, don't turn directly away. Instead, drift toward the wall slightly before turning. This gives you more room on the other side.

### The Gap Jump

Some sections have gaps in the slope. You don't actually need to jump - just steer toward the gap and the ball will naturally cross it if you have enough speed.

### The Tunnel Vision Cure

When the game gets fast, players tend to focus on a narrow area. Consciously widen your focus to see the entire slope width.

## Practice Schedule

To improve fast, play 5-10 rounds daily. Focus on one technique per session. Don't worry about score - focus on executing techniques correctly.

## Common Mistakes

1. Overcorrecting after a near-miss
2. Panicking when the speed increases
3. Watching the ball instead of the path ahead
4. Holding one direction too long

## Conclusion

Slope rewards patience and practice. The more you play, the better your muscle memory becomes. Stay calm, look ahead, and you'll be hitting high scores in no time. Play Slope free on PlayOnHub!

title: Drift Hunters: Beginner's Guide to Perfect Drifting
excerpt: New to Drift Hunters? This guide covers everything from basic drifting techniques to car upgrades and track strategies.
content:
Drift Hunters is the most comprehensive browser drifting game available. With dozens of cars, multiple tracks, and deep customization, there's a lot to learn. This guide will get you drifting like a pro.

## Getting Started

When you first launch Drift Hunters, you'll start with a basic car and a small amount of cash. Your goal is to drift, earn points, and upgrade your car or buy new ones.

## Basic Drifting Technique

1. **Approach a corner at moderate speed** - not too fast, not too slow
2. **Steer into the turn** while tapping the handbrake (Shift)
3. **Counter-steer** to maintain the drift angle
4. **Modulate the throttle** to control the drift
5. **Exit smoothly** by straightening the wheel and applying full throttle

## Scoring System

Drift points are based on:
- **Drift angle**: Higher angles earn more points
- **Speed**: Faster drifts score more
- **Duration**: Longer drifts multiply your score
- **Combo**: Chain drifts without straightening for combo multipliers

## Car Upgrades Priority

When upgrading your car, follow this priority:

1. **Power**: More horsepower means easier to break traction
2. **Tires**: Better grip for controlled drifts
3. **Weight**: Lighter cars are more responsive
4. **Gearbox**: Shorter ratios for better acceleration

## Best Beginner Cars

- **Nissan Silvia S15**: Balanced and easy to drift
- **Toyota AE86**: Light and nimble, great for learning
- **BMW M3**: Powerful and predictable

## Track Tips

### Port Arena

Wide open spaces, perfect for beginners. Practice long sweeping drifts here.

### Industrial

Tight corners require quick transitions. Good for practicing combo drifts.

### Mountain Pass

Long winding roads with elevation changes. The ultimate test of drift control.

## Common Mistakes

- **Too much throttle**: Spinning out is the most common mistake. Learn to modulate.
- **Not enough speed**: You need momentum to maintain a drift.
- **Wrong gear**: Stay in 2nd or 3rd gear for most drifts.
- **Giving up on a drift**: If you start to spin, counter-steer harder instead of braking.

## Conclusion

Drifting takes practice, but Drift Hunters makes it fun to learn. Start slow, focus on technique over score, and you'll be chaining massive drift combos in no time. Play Drift Hunters free on PlayOnHub!
keywords: how to drift in drift hunters

title: Shell Shockers: Every Weapon Class Ranked and Explained
excerpt: Confused about which weapon to use in Shell Shockers? This complete weapon guide breaks down every class with pros, cons, and strategies.
content:
Shell Shockers offers 7 unique weapon classes, each with distinct playstyles. Choosing the right weapon can make or break your match. Here's the complete breakdown.

## 1. EggK-47 (Assault Rifle)

The most versatile weapon in the game. Balanced damage, fire rate, and range make it perfect for beginners and pros alike.

**Best for**: All-around gameplay, medium range
**Pros**: Versatile, easy to use, good damage
**Cons**: Not the best at any one thing

## 2. Tri-Hard (Shotgun)

Devastating at close range, useless at distance. The Tri-Hard fires 3 pellets per shot.

**Best for**: Close quarters, aggressive play
**Pros**: Massive close-range damage
**Cons**: Very short effective range, slow fire rate

## 3. Crackshot (Sniper)

One-shot kill potential at any range, but requires precise aim. The ultimate skill weapon.

**Best for**: Long range, patient players
**Pros**: One-shot kills, long range
**Cons**: Slow fire rate, requires accuracy, bad at close range

## 4. Whipper (SMG)

High fire rate with moderate damage. Great for aggressive players who like to rush.

**Best for**: Close to medium range, rushing
**Pros**: Fast fire rate, good mobility
**Cons**: Damage falls off at range, burns through ammo

## 5. RPEGG (Rocket Launcher)

Explosive splash damage weapon. Great for groups but slow to reload.

**Best for**: Area denial, group fights
**Pros**: Splash damage, suppressive fire
**Cons**: Slow reload, direct hits required for kills

## 6. Tri-Hard (Double Barrel)

Two shots before reload, massive damage up close. High risk, high reward.

**Best for**: Close range ambush
**Pros**: Two quick shots, high damage
**Cons**: Only 2 shots, long reload

## 7. Scrambler

Short-range energy weapon with rapid fire. Good for beginners learning movement.

**Best for**: Very close range
**Pros**: No ammo management, fast fire
**Cons**: Very short range, low damage per hit

## Choosing Your Weapon

For beginners, start with the **EggK-47**. It's forgiving and teaches you the fundamentals. Once you're comfortable, experiment with other weapons to find your playstyle.

For competitive play, the **Crackshot** and **EggK-47** are the most popular choices at high skill levels.

## Conclusion

Each weapon in Shell Shockers has a niche. Master one before moving to the next, and you'll become a well-rounded player. Play Shell Shockers free on PlayOnHub!

title: How to Play IO Games at School: Complete Guide
excerpt: School blocking your favorite games? Here's everything you need to know about playing .io games safely and responsibly at school.
content:
Many students want to play games during breaks or free periods, but school networks often block gaming sites. Here's a guide to playing .io games at school responsibly.

## Why Schools Block Games

Schools block gaming sites for several reasons:
- To keep students focused during class
- To conserve bandwidth
- To comply with internet safety policies
- To prevent distractions during study time

## Responsible Gaming at School

### Only Play During Free Time

Never play games during class. Only play during lunch breaks, study halls, or free periods when you have no assignments due.

### Use Headphones

If you're playing games in a shared space, use headphones to avoid disturbing others.

### Keep Volume Low

Even with headphones, keep the volume at a reasonable level so you can hear if a teacher calls you.

## Games That Work Well at School

### Quick Games (5-10 minutes)

- [2048](/games/2048): A quiet puzzle game that doesn't require sound
- Paper.io 2: Quick rounds, easy to pause

### Multiplayer Games

- [Shell Shockers](/games/shell-shockers): Fast-paced FPS rounds
- [Krunker.io](/games/krunker-io): Quick match-based gameplay
- [Smash Karts](/games/smash-karts): Fun kart battles
- [Kour.io](/games/kour-io): Exciting FPS with multiple game modes
- [EvoWars.io](/games/evowars-io): Battle arena with evolution mechanics

### Quiet Games

- [Agar.io](/games/agar-io): Minimal sound required
- [Diep.io](/games/diepio): Strategic tank game
- [2048](/games/2048): Completely silent

## Tips for Playing at School

1. **Check Your School's Policy**: Some schools allow gaming during designated times
2. **Use School-Approved Breaks**: Only play during officially sanctioned free time
3. **Don't Download Anything**: Browser games don't require downloads, which is safer
4. **Be Respectful**: If asked to stop, stop immediately
5. **Prioritize Schoolwork**: Games should never come before assignments

## Why Browser Games Are Better for School

Browser games like those on PlayOnHub have advantages over downloaded games:

- No installation required
- No files stored on school computers
- Nothing to uninstall when done
- Works on Chromebooks and school laptops
- No admin access needed

## Alternative: Educational Games

If your school blocks all gaming sites, try educational alternatives:
- Typing games that improve keyboard skills
- Math puzzle games like [2048](/games/2048)
- Word games that build vocabulary like [Hangman](/games/hangman)

## Conclusion

Playing games at school can be a great way to relax during breaks, as long as you do it responsibly. Always follow your school's rules and prioritize your education. When you have free time, PlayOnHub has 30+ free browser games ready to play!

title: Slope Unblocked: How to Play at School and Work in 2026
excerpt: Want to play Slope unblocked at school or work? Here's everything you need to know about accessing Slope, plus tips for high scores.
content:
Slope is one of the most searched browser games in the world, and 'slope unblocked' is among the highest-traffic keywords in the browser gaming space. Here's your complete guide to playing Slope unblocked.

## What is Slope?

Slope is a 3D endless runner where you control a ball speeding down a neon slope. The ball accelerates continuously, and you must dodge red obstacles and avoid falling off the edges. The longer you survive, the faster it gets.

## How to Play Slope Unblocked

Many schools and workplaces block gaming sites. Here's how to play Slope unblocked:

### Method 1: Use PlayOnHub

PlayOnHub hosts Slope and many other games. Simply visit playonhub.com and search for Slope. Our site is frequently updated to remain accessible.

### Method 2: Google Sites

Some users create Google Sites pages with embedded Slope games. Search 'slope unblocked google sites' to find working links.

### Method 3: Browser Extensions

Some VPN extensions can help bypass network restrictions. However, always follow your school or workplace's internet policies.

## Slope Controls

- **Left Arrow / A**: Move left
- **Right Arrow / D**: Move right
- **Space**: Pause (in some versions)

The controls are simple, but the gameplay is incredibly challenging at high speeds.

## Tips for High Scores

### 1. Stay Centered

The center of the slope gives you the most reaction time. Only move to the sides when you need to dodge an obstacle.

### 2. Make Micro-Movements

Sharp turns at high speed will send you off the edge. Make small, gentle adjustments to your trajectory.

### 3. Look Ahead

Don't watch the ball - watch what's coming. Anticipation is the key to surviving at high speeds. Your brain needs time to process obstacles before you reach them.

### 4. Stay Calm Under Pressure

As the speed increases, panic is your biggest enemy. Keep your breathing steady and your movements deliberate. Panic leads to overcorrection, which leads to falling off.

### 5. Practice Regularly

Slope is a muscle memory game. Play 5-10 rounds daily. Your brain will adapt to the speed and your reactions will become automatic.

## Common Obstacles

- **Red Blocks**: Stationary obstacles you must dodge
- **Gaps**: Missing sections of the slope - steer around them
- **Tunnels**: Narrow passages requiring precise positioning
- **Moving Blocks**: Obstacles that shift position

## Why Slope is So Addictive

Slope uses a perfect difficulty curve. The game starts easy, giving you confidence, then gradually increases speed until you're fighting for survival. Each run feels beatable, which keeps you coming back for 'one more try.'

## Slope Variants

The success of Slope has spawned many variants:
- **Slope 2**: Sequel with new obstacles and mechanics
- **Slope Ball**: Ball physics variant
- **Slope Rainbow**: Colorful visual variant
- **Run 3**: Similar endless runner concept

## Conclusion

Slope unblocked is the perfect quick game - easy to learn, hard to master, and playable anywhere. Whether you're at school, work, or home, PlayOnHub has Slope ready to play. No downloads, no registration, just pure arcade fun.

title: 1v1.LOL Unblocked: Complete Guide to Building and Shooting
excerpt: Master 1v1.LOL with our complete guide. Learn building techniques, shooting tips, and how to play unblocked at school.
content:
1v1.LOL is one of the most popular unblocked browser games, combining Fortnite-style building mechanics with competitive 1v1 combat. Here's everything you need to know.

## What is 1v1.LOL?

1v1.LOL is a third-person shooter where you can build structures while fighting. Think of it as Fortnite's building mechanics distilled into a browser-based 1v1 format. You can practice against bots, play against friends, or join multiplayer matches.

## How to Play 1v1.LOL Unblocked

'1v1.lol unblocked' is one of the highest-searched browser game keywords. Here's how to access it:

### Play on PlayOnHub

Visit playonhub.com and search for 1v1.LOL. We host the game directly, so you can play without any restrictions.

### Why is 1v1.LOL Blocked?

Many schools block gaming sites to keep students focused. However, 1v1.LOL is also a legitimate practice tool for Fortnite players who want to improve their building skills.

## Controls

### Movement
- **WASD**: Move
- **Space**: Jump
- **Shift**: Sprint

### Combat
- **Mouse**: Aim
- **Left Click**: Shoot
- **R**: Reload
- **1-4**: Switch weapons

### Building
- **Z**: Wall
- **X**: Ramp
- **C**: Floor
- **V**: Roof
- **Right Click**: Edit structure

## Building Techniques

### The Wall Ramp Rush

The most fundamental building technique:
1. Place a wall in front of you
2. Place a ramp behind it
3. Jump on the ramp
4. Place another wall and ramp
5. Repeat to gain height advantage

### The 90s Technique

Advanced height-gaining technique:
1. Place a wall and ramp
2. Turn 90 degrees
3. Place another wall and ramp
4. Turn 90 degrees again
5. Repeat to quickly gain vertical position

### Defensive Boxing

When under fire:
1. Place walls on all four sides
2. Place a roof on top
3. Edit a window to peek and shoot
4. Reset the edit to block incoming fire

## Shooting Tips

### Aim Training

Use the training mode to practice your aim. Focus on:
- Tracking moving targets
- Flick shots for quick aim adjustment
- Headshots for maximum damage

### Weapon Selection

- **Assault Rifle**: Versatile, good at all ranges
- **Shotgun**: Devastating up close, best for build battles
- **Sniper**: Long-range picks, requires good positioning
- **SMG**: Fast fire rate, great for suppressing fire

## Game Modes

### 1v1 Mode

Classic 1v1 against another player or bot. Best for practicing building and editing.

### Box Fight Mode

Close-quarters combat in small boxes. Tests your editing and close-range aim.

### Just Build Mode

Practice building without combat pressure. Great for learning new techniques.

### Battle Royale

Multiplayer mode where you fight to be the last player standing.

## Pro Tips

1. **Always have a wall ready**: Defensive walls save lives. Get into the habit of walling up when taking fire.
2. **Edit speed matters**: The faster you can edit, the more unpredictable you become. Practice editing in Just Build mode.
3. **High ground wins**: In build battles, the player with the high ground has a significant advantage.
4. **Don't tunnel vision**: Keep track of your opponent's position. Many players get so focused on building that they forget to watch for pushes.
5. **Learn to reset edits**: If you edit a window to shoot, reset it immediately. Leaving an opening is a free shot for your opponent.

## Conclusion

1v1.LOL is the best browser game for practicing building and editing skills. Whether you're a Fortnite player looking to improve or just enjoy competitive shooters, 1v1.LOL offers deep, rewarding gameplay. Play 1v1.LOL unblocked on PlayOnHub - no downloads needed!

title: Agar.io Private Server: How to Create and Join Custom Servers
excerpt: Learn how to find, join, and create Agar.io private servers. Play with friends, use custom rules, and dominate the arena.
content:
Agar.io is the game that started the .io game craze. While the official servers are fun, private servers offer custom game modes, unlimited mass, and the ability to play with friends. Here's everything you need to know.

## What is an Agar.io Private Server?

A private server is a custom Agar.io server that runs independently from the official game. Private servers can have:
- Custom game modes
- Modified mechanics (faster splitting, bigger starting mass)
- Custom skins and themes
- Password-protected rooms for friends
- Bot players for practice

## How to Find Agar.io Private Servers

### Method 1: Play on PlayOnHub

PlayOnHub hosts Agar.io directly - you can start playing immediately without searching for server lists.

### Method 2: Agario Server Lists

Several websites maintain lists of active private servers. Search 'agar.io private server list' on Google to find current options.

### Method 3: Agario Custom Servers

Some communities run custom servers with unique mods. Check Discord communities and Reddit (r/Agario) for server invites.

## Popular Private Server Modes

### FFA (Free For All)

Classic mode where everyone fights everyone. The largest cell dominates the server.

### Teams Mode

Players are divided into teams. Work together to consume opposing team members.

### Battle Royale

Last cell standing wins. The play area shrinks over time, forcing encounters.

### Experimental Mode

Servers with modified mechanics like faster movement, bigger splits, or unusual starting conditions.

## How to Create Your Own Private Server

### Option 1: Use Agario Clone Scripts

Several open-source Agar.io clone scripts are available on GitHub. These let you host your own server with custom rules.

### Option 2: Rent a Game Server

Some hosting providers offer pre-configured Agar.io server hosting. This is the easiest option but costs money.

### Option 3: Host Locally

You can run a server on your own computer using Node.js. This is free but only works for local network play unless you set up port forwarding.

## Agar.io Pro Tips

### Splitting Strategy

Splitting (Space bar) sends half your mass forward. Use it to:
- Catch smaller cells that are running away
- Surprise attack from distance
- Escape from larger cells by splitting into pieces

### Virus Tactics

Green viruses (spiky cells) can be used offensively:
- Feed mass into a virus (W key) to shoot it at large cells
- If a virus hits a large cell, it splits them into many pieces
- Use viruses as cover when being chased

### Edge Camping

Stay near the map edge when small. Larger cells can't circle around you, reducing the angles of attack.

### Team Up

In FFA mode, informal teams often form. Feed mass to each other (W key) to build trust, then work together to dominate the server.

## Common Mistakes

1. **Splitting recklessly**: Only split when you're sure you can consume the target. After splitting, you're vulnerable.
2. **Ignoring viruses**: Viruses are your best weapon against larger cells. Learn to use them.
3. **Chasing too far**: Don't chase a small cell across the map. You'll lose mass and expose yourself to ambushes.
4. **Not ejecting mass**: Ejecting mass (W) is useful for feeding teammates, baiting enemies, and shooting viruses.

## Conclusion

Agar.io private servers add a new dimension to the classic game. Whether you want custom modes, friend-only rooms, or just a fresh experience, private servers are worth exploring. For instant play, visit PlayOnHub and start eating cells right now!

title: 2048 Strategy Guide: Proven Tactics to Reach the 4096 Tile
excerpt: Stuck at 1024? Learn the proven strategies that will help you reach 2048, 4096, and beyond in this comprehensive guide.
content:
2048 is a deceptively simple puzzle game that has captivated millions of players. The rules are easy: combine matching numbers to double their value. But reaching the 2048 tile - let alone 4096 or 8192 - requires strategy and foresight.

## The Golden Rule: Pick a Corner

The single most important strategy in 2048 is to keep your highest-value tile in a corner. Most players choose the bottom-right corner, but any corner works.

### Why the Corner Strategy Works

By keeping your highest tile in a corner, you create a descending chain of values along one edge. This makes it easy to combine tiles in order, from smallest to largest.

### The Corner Strategy Step by Step

1. Choose a corner (let's say bottom-right)
2. Only swipe in 3 directions: down, right, and left (or up, right, and left)
3. NEVER swipe in the direction that would move your highest tile out of the corner
4. Build a chain: highest tile in corner, next highest next to it, and so on

## The Chain Strategy

Your goal is to create a descending chain along one edge of the board. For example: Row 1: 2-4-8-16, Row 2: 4-8-16-32, Row 3: 8-16-32-64, Row 4: 16-32-64-512.

## Advanced Strategies

### The Three-Move Rule

Only use three of the four swipe directions. If your highest tile is in the bottom-right corner:
- **Use**: Down, Left, Right
- **Never use**: Up

Using 'up' would move your highest tile out of the corner, potentially ruining your chain.

### Forced Up Moves

Sometimes the game forces you to swipe up because no other moves are available. To minimize this:
- Keep the bottom row as full as possible
- Avoid creating gaps in the bottom row
- Plan ahead to ensure you always have a valid down or left/right move

### The Two-Column Strategy

Instead of using the full board, focus on two columns:
1. Keep your main chain in the rightmost column
2. Use the second column as a buffer for new tiles
3. Combine tiles in the buffer column before merging them into the main chain

### Tile Management

- **Merge small tiles first**: Don't let small tiles (2, 4) accumulate. Merge them into 8s and 16s quickly.
- **Plan two moves ahead**: Before swiping, think about what tiles will appear and where they'll end up.
- **Avoid random tiles in your chain**: If a small tile appears in your chain, work around it or merge it out.

## Common Mistakes

### 1. Swiping in All Directions

The #1 mistake. Randomly swiping in all four directions scatters your tiles and prevents chain formation. Stick to three directions.

### 2. Chasing Big Combines

Don't focus on making one big combine. Focus on building and maintaining your chain. Big combines happen naturally when your chain is organized.

### 3. Panicking When the Corner Tile Moves

If your highest tile gets knocked out of the corner, don't panic. Immediately work to get it back. Avoid swiping in the problematic direction until it's fixed.

### 4. Ignoring the Board State

Before each swipe, look at the entire board. What will happen to each tile? Where will new tiles appear? Thoughtful play beats reactive play.

## Scoring System

- Merging two tiles gives you points equal to the new tile's value
- Merging two 2s = 4 points
- Merging two 1024s = 2048 points
- Higher merges = exponentially more points

## How to Reach 4096 and Beyond

Reaching 2048 is achievable with the corner strategy. Going further requires:

1. **Perfect chain management**: Your descending chain must be flawless
2. **Patience**: Games to 4096+ can take 30+ minutes
3. **No forced up moves**: Every time you're forced to swipe up, your chain is disrupted
4. **Buffer column mastery**: Use your buffer column efficiently to feed tiles into the chain

## Conclusion

2048 is a game of strategy, not luck. By mastering the corner strategy, maintaining a descending chain, and only using three swipe directions, you can consistently reach 2048 and beyond. Play 2048 free on PlayOnHub and put these strategies to the test!
